tool_call.name: retrieve_information
tool_call.args: {"prompt":"In {{spacex_s1}}, find any information about the IPO price range, the number of shares being offered, the underwriters, and any discussion of the offering structure. Also look for the cover page details about shares offered and price range."}

parsed_args.prompt: In {{spacex_s1}}, find any information about the IPO price range, the number of shares being offered, the underwriters, and any discussion of the offering structure. Also look for the cover page details about shares offered and price range.

tool_output.output:
Based on the provided prospectus excerpts, here is the information regarding the IPO price range, shares offered, underwriters, offering structure, and cover page details:

### 1. IPO Price Range & Number of Shares Offered
While the specific numbers are redacted (blanks) in the main prospectus text, the S-1 fee table provides explicit figures:
*   **Number of Shares Offered:** **66,666,670** shares of Class A common stock. 
*   **IPO Price Range:** The fee table outlines proposed maximum offering prices escalating from **$5.5 trillion to $7.5 trillion** in aggregate. Dividing this aggregate amount by the 66,666,670 shares offered indicates an estimated price range of approximately **$82,500 to $112,500 per share**. 
*   **Over-Allotment Option:** The underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional (redacted) number of shares of Class A common stock.

### 2. Underwriters
The IPO is being underwritten by a large syndicate of financial institutions. The lead bookrunning managers and co-managers include:
*   **Lead Underwriters:** Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC, BofA Securities, Inc., Citigroup Global Markets Inc., and J.P. Morgan Securities LLC.
*   **Additional Underwriters:** Barclays Capital Inc., Deutsche Bank Securities Inc., RBC Capital Markets, LLC, UBS Securities LLC, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC, Banco BTG Pactual S.A. – Cayman Branch, ING Bank N.V., Allen & Company LLC, Cantor, Needham & Company, Raymond James, Societe Generale, Stifel, William Blair, Macquarie Capital, Mirae Asset Securities Co., Ltd., Mizuho, and Santander.
*   *Note:* ING Bank N.V., Banco BTG Pactual S.A. – Cayman Branch, and Mirae Asset Securities Co., Ltd. are noted as not being broker-dealers registered with the SEC, restricting their direct sales in the U.S. 

### 3. Offering Structure
The offering features several distinct structural components:
*   **Dual-Class Voting Structure:** The company will have Class A and Class B common stock. Class A shareholders get 1 vote per share, while Class B shareholders get 10 votes per share. Class B shareholders will vote together with Class A as a single class, except they have the exclusive right to elect a majority of the board of directors.
*   **Pre-Offering Reclassifications:** Prior to the IPO, all outstanding Class C common stock will be reclassified into Class A common stock, and all preferred stock will convert into Class A and Class B common stock.
*   **Directed Share Program (DSP):** Up to a (redacted) percent of shares are reserved for sale to certain employees, management associates, friends, and family. These shares will not be subject to lock-up restrictions.
*   **Retail Investor Allocation:** A portion of shares will be offered to retail investors through specific online brokerage platforms, including Charles Schwab, Fidelity, Robinhood, SoFi Securities, and E*TRADE.
*   **Lock-Up Agreements:** Existing shareholders are subject to a staggered early release schedule based on post-IPO dates, earnings releases, and stock price performance (e.g., releasing 20% after the first earnings release, with more released if the stock trades 30% above the IPO price). The Founder is excluded from these early release provisions.
*   **Stabilization:** Morgan Stanley will act as stabilization agent and may engage in covered and naked short sales to stabilize the price of the Class A common stock post-IPO.

### 4. Cover Page Details
*   **Issuer:** Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (a Texas corporation).
*   **Listing:** The Class A common stock has been applied to be listed on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (“Nasdaq”) and Nasdaq Texas, Inc. under the trading symbol **“SPCX.”**
*   **Shares Outstanding Post-Offering:** The cover page details the outstanding shares of Class A and Class B common stock immediately after the offering (blanks in the text), alongside the respective voting power percentages of each class. 
*   **Use of Proceeds:** Net proceeds are intended to fund the company's growth strategy, specifically the expansion of AI compute infrastructure, enhancements to launch infrastructure and vehicles, increases in satellite constellation scale/capacity, and general corporate purposes.
